summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, and cloud-account data to attacker endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs on every `npm install` and harvests installer-owned identity and cloud-context data, then POSTs it as JSON to a hardcoded remote endpoint at https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Collected fields include the host's hostname and username, ~/.gitconfig user.email,.git/config and.git/logs/HEAD reflog committer emails, the comment fields of every ~/.ssh/*.pub file, GitHub CLI host identity from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf, parent project package.json metadata, and CI environment indicators. The package name and repository (`anthropic-toolkit`, `github.com/anthropic-toolkit/anthropic-toolkit`) impersonate the Anthropic brand and declare a peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk to attract installs from developers using Anthropic's SDK. A README comment frames the collection as 'anonymous diagnostics' with an ANTHROPIC_TOOLKIT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED opt-out, but the fields collected (SCM email, SSH key comments, GCP account email, reflog authors, hostname, username) are personally identifying and unrelated to compatibility diagnostics — the cover story does not match the data.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
